description: US airstrike kills Ali Khamenei, Supreme Leader of Iran
Following assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei, Iran enters open conflict with Israel and US forces in the region
Al-Qaeda hijackers crash four airliners in coordinated attacks on US soil, killing nearly 3,000 people
US-led coalition invades Iraq citing WMD threat; coalition forces reach Baghdad within three weeks
Hurricane Katrina devastates Gulf Coast; catastrophic federal response to flooding of majority-Black New Orleans neighborhoods exposes racial inequality in disaster relief
Lehman Brothers collapse triggers global financial crisis; unemployment peaks at 10% and millions lose homes
Affordable Care Act signed into law, expanding Medicaid and creating individual health insurance mandate
US Navy SEALs kill Osama bin Laden in Abbottabad, Pakistan, in covert raid authorized by President Obama
Trayvon Martin shot and killed by George Zimmerman in Sanford, Florida; acquittal sparks national debate on race and self-defense
Supreme Court upholds Affordable Care Act in NFIB v. Sebelius, ruling individual mandate constitutional as a tax

Pre-election Twitter activity, September 2012 (jgoodman8 Kaggle dataset, Sept 13-14 2012, ~1M tweets). Not Election Day (Nov 6 2012) — archive covers the campaign period only. Baseline pre-Trump election for sentiment calibration.

archive_schema_notes.user_description: verification required on first load — determines Tier 1 vs Tier 2

description: Gunman kills 20 children and 6 staff at Sandy Hook Elementary School in Newtown, Connecticut; deadliest school shooting in US history
Tsarnaev brothers detonate pressure-cooker bombs near Boston Marathon finish line, killing 3 and injuring 264; manhunt ends days later
NYPD officer Daniel Pantaleo kills Eric Garner with chokehold during arrest; 'I can't breathe' becomes a national rallying cry
Police officer Darren Wilson shoots and kills Michael Brown in Ferguson, Missouri, igniting weeks of protests and DOJ civil rights investigation
Supreme Court rules in Obergefell v. Hodges that same-sex couples have a constitutional right to marry nationwide
P5+1 and Iran reach Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action limiting Iran's nuclear program in exchange for sanctions relief
ISIS coordinated attacks across Paris kill 130 people at concert hall, restaurants, and national stadium; deadliest attack on France since WWII
Omar Mateen kills 49 people at Pulse LGBTQ nightclub in Orlando; deadliest mass shooting in US history at the time, targeting Latino LGBTQ community on Latin Night
Minnesota officer Jeronimo Yanez fatally shoots Philando Castile during traffic stop while girlfriend livestreams on Facebook; acquittal inflames BLM movement
Washington Post publishes 2005 Access Hollywood tape of Trump boasting about sexually assaulting women
Comey letter to Congress announcing FBI reopening Clinton email investigation 11 days before election
Donald Trump defeats Hillary Clinton in Electoral College upset despite losing popular vote by nearly 3 million; kinguistics Kaggle dataset covers Election Day tweets

archive_schema_notes.user_description: verification required on first load — determines Tier 1 vs Tier 2

description: Trump signs executive order banning travel from seven Muslim-majority countries and suspending refugee admissions
Trump announces US withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement, calling it unfair to American workers
White supremacist Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville kills one counter-protester; Trump says there were 'very fine people on both sides'
Trump administration announces end of DACA program, placing approximately 700,000 Dreamers at risk of deportation
Stephen Paddock kills 60 concertgoers and wounds 413 from his Mandalay Bay hotel room at the Route 91 Harvest festival — deadliest mass shooting in modern US history

Viral #MeToo movement exposing widespread sexual harassment and assault following Harvey Weinstein allegations; covered by Foster & Rathlin 2022 LIWC dataset (3,683 tweets, DOI: 10.5683/SP3/1YWCW1) and Reddit Pushshift (TwoXChromosomes, Feminism, AskFeminists, AskWomen) filtered to Oct 15–Dec 31 2017

Christine Blasey Ford testifies before Senate Judiciary Committee accusing Supreme Court nominee Brett Kavanaugh of sexual assault; Kavanaugh confirmed October 6; Foster & Rathlin 2022 dataset covers kavanaugh_ford and kavanaugh_confirmed phases
Nikolas Cruz kills 17 students and staff at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida; student-led March for Our Lives movement demands gun control
Trump zero-tolerance policy separates migrant children from parents at US-Mexico border; over 2,500 children taken
Gunman kills 11 worshippers at Tree of Life synagogue in Pittsburgh, largest antisemitic attack in US history
White nationalist Patrick Crusius kills 23 people at El Paso Walmart in attack targeting Latinos; manifesto echoes Trump immigration rhetoric
House Speaker Pelosi announces formal impeachment inquiry into Trump's Ukraine phone call pressuring Zelensky to investigate Biden

COVID-19 summer surge discourse, July–August 2020; gpreda Kaggle archive covers this window. Conceptually renamed covid_summer_2020 — id kept as covid_pandemic_2020 to preserve archive_ids references.
FDA grants Emergency Use Authorization for Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine, beginning US vaccination rollout; ~200k tweets from kaushiksuresh147 Kaggle dataset with user_description confirmed

archive_schema_notes.user_description: user_description (confirmed present — use for bio classification)

description: Minneapolis police officer Derek Chauvin kills George Floyd; largest protest movement in US history erupts across hundreds of cities
archive_schema_notes: Twitter BLM 2020 coverage dropped 2026-06-03 — all candidates are IDs-only, wrong language, or wrong time period. Sole source: Reddit Pushshift r/BlackLivesMatter + r/BlackPeopleTwitter, filtered to 2020-05-25 through 2020-08-31, inference_method=subreddit_proxy, exclude from Sigma_Delta.

description: Supreme Court blocks Trump's DACA rescission 5-4, ruling the termination arbitrary and capricious under the APA
Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg dies at 87; Republicans move to fill the seat weeks before the presidential election, sparking court-packing debate
Trump tests positive for COVID-19 and is hospitalized at Walter Reed; reveals White House superspreader event from Amy Coney Barrett announcement
Joe Biden defeats Donald Trump in presidential election, winning Electoral College 306-232 and popular vote by 7 million; sinking8 Kaggle dataset covers election-week tweets

archive_schema_notes.user_description: verification required on first load — determines Tier 1 vs Tier 2

description: Trump supporters storm US Capitol to halt certification of Biden's 2020 Electoral College victory; five die, over 140 officers injured
Derek Chauvin convicted on all three counts in murder of George Floyd — first Minnesota police officer convicted of murder in the line of duty
Republican-led legislative and school board battles against Critical Race Theory teaching spread to 42 states; becomes proxy for White backlash to racial reckoning
Taliban captures Kabul as US completes withdrawal from Afghanistan; chaotic evacuation scenes at Hamid Karzai International Airport
Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine; largest land war in Europe since World War II
US CPI reaches 9.1% year-over-year in June 2022 — highest inflation in 40 years — driven by energy, food, and shelter costs; Fed begins aggressive rate hikes
Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade in Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization, returning abortion regulation to states
Trump indicted on 37 federal counts related to classified documents at Mar-a-Lago — first federal criminal indictment of a former US president
Supreme Court strikes down race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and UNC, effectively ending affirmative action in higher education
Hamas launches surprise attack on southern Israel killing 1,200 people; Israel declares war and begins siege of Gaza
New York jury finds Trump guilty on all 34 felony counts of falsifying business records; first US president convicted of a felony crime

Gunman shoots Trump at Butler, Pennsylvania rally; bullet grazes his ear as Secret Service kills shooter; Thomas Matthew Crooks identified as attacker; covered by kashish-s USC Humans Lab Truth Social dataset

Biden announces he will not seek reelection and endorses VP Kamala Harris — first incumbent president to withdraw from a re-election campaign since LBJ in 1968; covered by kashish-s USC Humans Lab Truth Social dataset

Donald Trump defeats Kamala Harris in presidential election, winning Electoral College and popular vote. truth_social_2024 (kashish-s USC Humans Lab) coverage ends 2024-10-29, seven days before election day — shock_date is set to that last-coverage date for Truth Social sampling. Reddit (reddit_pushshift) and Telegram (telegram_2024) extend through the post-election window to 2024-11-19.
